title: Financial Inclusion Mastery Checkpoint
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you continue developing your financial knowledge and discipline?
What specific steps will you take to participate responsibly in financial systems?

stages.prompt: Task: Demonstrate safe financial behaviour across multiple scenarios. Which approach shows readiness for formal financial participation?

options.label: Taking financial risks without understanding consequences
options.reflection: Taking financial risks without understanding consequences shows unpreparedness for formal financial participation. This approach can lead to serious financial problems.

options.label: Using knowledge to make informed financial decisions
options.reflection: Exactly! Using knowledge to make informed financial decisions shows readiness for formal financial participation. Understanding the implications of your choices is key to safe participation.

options.label: Copying others' financial decisions without analysis
options.reflection: Copying others' financial decisions without analysis is risky. Everyone's financial situation is different, so you need to understand the rationale behind decisions.

options.label: Avoiding all financial services to stay safe
options.reflection: Avoiding all financial services prevents you from building financial experience and accessing benefits of the formal system. Learning to navigate safely is better than avoiding entirely.

stages.prompt: How do you demonstrate responsible borrowing behavior?

options.label: Assessing repayment capacity before borrowing
options.reflection: Perfect! Assessing repayment capacity before borrowing demonstrates responsible borrowing behavior. This approach ensures you don't overextend financially.

options.label: Borrowing the maximum amount offered
options.reflection: Borrowing the maximum amount offered without considering your repayment capacity is irresponsible. Responsible borrowing means only borrowing what you can comfortably repay.

options.label: Borrowing for any urgent need regardless of cost
options.reflection: Borrowing for any urgent need without considering the cost and your ability to repay is not responsible. Even urgent needs should be evaluated against your financial capacity.

options.label: Taking multiple loans from different sources
options.reflection: Taking multiple loans without careful consideration can lead to over-indebtedness. Responsible borrowing requires understanding your total debt burden.

stages.prompt: What indicates financial discipline in managing credit?

options.label: Maximizing credit utilization to build credit history
options.reflection: Maximizing credit utilization is not a sign of discipline. High utilization can negatively impact your credit score and indicate financial stress.

options.label: Paying only the minimum amount due
options.reflection: Paying only the minimum amount due can lead to accumulating high interest charges. True discipline involves paying more than the minimum when possible.

options.label: Avoiding credit entirely
options.reflection: While avoiding credit might seem safe, it doesn't build credit history. Managing credit responsibly demonstrates discipline and builds positive credit history.

options.label: Making payments on time and keeping balances low
options.reflection: Excellent! Making payments on time and keeping balances low indicates financial discipline in managing credit. This demonstrates responsible credit management.

stages.prompt: How do you verify the legitimacy of a financial service provider?

options.label: Based on attractive advertisements
options.reflection: Attractive advertisements don't guarantee legitimacy. Scammers often use appealing ads to lure victims. Verification requires checking official credentials.

options.label: By how quickly they approve applications
options.reflection: Fast approval doesn't indicate legitimacy. Legitimate providers follow proper verification procedures. Be wary of unusually fast approvals.

options.label: Checking official licenses and regulatory approvals
options.reflection: Exactly! Checking official licenses and regulatory approvals is how you verify the legitimacy of a financial service provider. This ensures you're dealing with a regulated entity.

options.label: Based solely on friend referrals
options.reflection: While referrals can be helpful, relying solely on them for verification isn't wise. Even well-meaning friends might not know about fraudulent schemes.

stages.prompt: What demonstrates financial preparedness for emergencies?

options.label: Reliance on credit cards for all emergencies
options.reflection: Reliance on credit cards for all emergencies shows poor preparedness. This approach can lead to debt accumulation and doesn't address the root issue of lacking savings.

options.label: Having dedicated emergency funds separate from regular expenses
options.reflection: Exactly! Having dedicated emergency funds separate from regular expenses demonstrates financial preparedness for emergencies. This provides a buffer without disrupting normal financial operations.

options.label: Ability to quickly borrow money when needed
options.reflection: Relying on borrowing for emergencies isn't true preparedness. Emergency funds provide more reliable and cost-effective protection than borrowing.

options.label: Only depending on insurance for protection
options.reflection: While insurance is important, depending only on it without emergency savings isn't complete preparedness. Both insurance and savings provide comprehensive protection.
